blocks.title: GeoGebra — Dynamic Geometry
blocks.html:
GeoGebra is a free, open-source dynamic mathematics software for all levels of education. It combines geometry, algebra, spreadsheets, graphing, statistics, and calculus in one tool.

Best for: Geometric investigations, exploring transformations, visualizing functions, constructing dynamic diagrams.

Website: https://www.geogebra.org/

blocks.title: Desmos — Graphing and Calculations
blocks.html:
Desmos is a free online graphing calculator and activity builder. It allows students to explore mathematical concepts through interactive graphs and activities.

Best for: Graphing equations, exploring function behavior, creating dynamic visualizations, classroom activities.

Website: https://www.desmos.com/

blocks.title: Spreadsheets — Data Organization
blocks.html:
Spreadsheets (Google Sheets, Microsoft Excel, LibreOffice Calc) are excellent for organizing, analyzing, and visualizing data.

Best for: Organizing collected data, creating tables and charts, performing calculations on large datasets, pattern identification.

blocks.title: Python/JavaScript — Programming Investigations
blocks.html:
Programming languages enable students to automate investigations, handle large computations, and build computational models.

Best for: Statistical modeling, simulation of mathematical scenarios, algorithmic investigations, data analysis and visualization.

blocks.title: Guidelines for Technology Use
items: Technology should support, not replace, mathematical thinking
Always verify technology-generated results with mathematical reasoning
Document the technology tools used in your investigation report
Consider accessibility — ensure all group members can use the chosen tools
Use screen captures and exports to include technology-based evidence in reports
